text: Having the patient positioned on his back and a right side laster with the aims of lifted up will ease the visualisation of the valve and with the right arm 45 degrees off the stretcher.
An oblique incision of about 3 cm on the right groin just above the femoral pulse is performed, minimizing an extensive dissection to avoid a defecting disruption.
The femoral artery and vein are dissected only superficially.
Then, pulse serine sutures are placed on both vessels with 4-O Proline and they are cannulated using Seldinger technique, always under echo control.
Once the guiding wire arrives up to the right, I should go ahead with the cannula insertion.
It is important to have an echo control by the time of the cannulation because they are going to guide and confirm the correct situation of the cannula.
Next, an aortic cannula is also placed by femoral artery canalization.
Be aware, if by the first time the guiding wires don't progress easily, don't insist.
Avoid this maneuver and try again from the beginning.
Once the artery canalization is finished, the arterial line is secured.

Going ahead with the minimally invasive setup and once the inframammary incision is done in the fourth intercostal space, the soft tissue retractor is placed and the low profile intercostal retractor is placed over this last one.
This is the incision for the aortic lamp.
And remember, care must be taken to avoid interference between the camera and the RT clamp.
For that, the camera should be inserted anterior and superior to the clamp with the aim to have a direct view onto the mitral valve.
This incision can be useful for getting the mediastinal drain out.
The last incision during the setup is for the atrial retractor.
It should be made on the third intercostal space over the parasternal line.
Finally, we can see on this picture the last setup configuration.
Once cardiac pulmonary bypass is established and the heart decompressed, the pericardium is opened 2 or 3 cm above and parallel to the phrenic nerve.
and sutures are placed at the SVC and AVC reflections and over the mid distance between these last ones and they are brought out percutaneously.
A string per stitch is made in preparation for the cardiopulmonary needle insertion.
The clamp should be directed towards ascending aorta without putting any force and taking care of the pulmonary artery and the left appendage.
Cardioplegia is infused.
The left atrium is opened along the underguard groove and the mitral retractor is placed.
Here we can see the mitral valve and having a complete examination.
Following with the valve replacement, a little incision over the anterior leaflet of the mitral valve is made and a correct exposure of the anterior annulus is achieved.
Then, stitches radiated from the outside to the inside are completed around the entire circumference of the mitral annulus.
Finally, both the anterior and the posterior leaflets are resected.
Sometimes we can preserve the subvalvular apparatus, but not in this case.
It is important to mention to be aware of the circumflex artery damage.
Remember the anatomy and its proximity to the posterior annulus.
Then we size the mitral valve anulus in order to select the correct valve and finally we replace the valve using core mount.
On the left, a trim is closed using a 4-0 Proline suture from both sides and they are tied in the middle.
